claim_summary: The dossier assesses the death and post-discovery investigation of the unknown man found at Somerton Beach on 1 December 1948 and separates evidence from unsupported claims.
notes: Discovery date is one of the highest-confidence findings in Mission 4.

claim_summary: The assessment is an intelligence-style case review governed by traceability across the project registers.
notes: No new factual case claim introduced.

claim_summary: The prior analytical dossier was treated as a claim source and checked against acquired records and OSINT updates.
The evidence hierarchy prioritizes official and contemporary records and preserves conflicts without assumption.
The repository contains source, verification, chronology, witness, victimology, forensic, physical evidence, intelligence, OSINT, vocabulary, and publication-readiness layers.
Mission 4 reviewed 75 claims: 6 verified, 14 partially verified, 31 unsupported, and 24 unable to verify.
Table summarizes discovery, chronology, identity, medical, physical evidence, Rubaiyat, and intelligence-linkage assessments.
notes: Table preserves confidence differences by area.

claim_summary: Strongest verified findings concern discovery, transport-ticket evidence, Tamam Shud slip association, and undetermined cause of death.
Many physical artefacts are documented but later custody is weak; Mission 9 records no complete or mostly complete physical-evidence custody trails.
notes: Physical-evidence existence stronger than custody continuity.

claim_summary: The Webb attribution is a strengthened researcher/lab-led identification lead pending official coroner/SAPOL determination.
verification_status: Supported OSINT update with official-status caveat

claim_summary: Cause and mechanism of death remain unresolved; common poison was not detected, but absence of all poison and place of death are not established.
notes: Preserves not detected vs not present distinction.

claim_summary: Final movements remain incomplete, including rail-ticket purchaser, sighting identity continuity, exact time of death, and place of death.
notes: No invented times or movement sequence.

claim_summary: Rubaiyat, Tamam Shud slip, and writing material are central evidence but unresolved in meaning and do not prove suicide, homicide, espionage, or a solved cipher.
verification_status: Supported by physical evidence and intelligence registers

claim_summary: Official identity status requires coroner, SAPOL, or Forensic Science SA documentation before Webb can be written as official identification.
Forensic-medical completeness remains a critical gap because full lab detail, retained-sample status, and modern review are missing.
Physical-evidence custody remains unresolved for key objects and samples.
No acquired source establishes administered poison, suicide note, assailant, intelligence-service connection, solved code, or complete last-movement reconstruction.
verification_status: Negative claim limited to acquired source base

claim_summary: The case is partly resolved at evidence level and unresolved at explanatory level.
Webb attribution does not close the case; cause remains undetermined; explanatory models remain hypotheses or models rather than conclusions.
The dossier's contribution is controlled evidence architecture rather than a new theory.
Chapter confidence is Moderate overall, with high confidence in selected core facts and very low confidence for direct intelligence involvement.
notes: Confidence reflects evidence quality, not theory probability.
